title: Casting
body:
As a youth community theatre, PYT believes **everyone deserves a role**. Everyone who auditions for a Main Stage production is cast - around 100 actors for each show across two casts. 

Even so, **casting is the hardest part of any production, for the actors, the parents, and the staff!** It can be incredibly tough to manage young actors' expectations, and their disappointment if they do not get the part they envisioned. BUT, there is a lot we at PYT can do, and that parents and actors can do, to make this as fair and as easy as it can be.

The first important thing to understand is that **most children will not get a leading role**, this is the nature and the numbers of any theatre production at any level. We hope to inspire all actors and parents to trust the casting team, who go to great lengths to make this process equitable, including removing any staff or adults from the process whose own children are auditioning.

PYT is **ensemble focused**, and our mission is to give as many young actors as much, and as positive an experience on stage as we can. So **we are pro-ensemble. We love ensemble!** Because we know this is how all actors grow, and that it provides the best opportunity for young actors to experience as wide as possible a breadth of the theatre and production. Every character with a non-leading role gets as much and as rich stage and rehearsal time as possible. We rework every show to make sure this is the case.

**As a community-focused theatre, we prioritize the following in any casting decisions**:

* How ready any actor is to take on a given role — **age, abilities, and experience come into play, but are not the overriding considerations.**
* **Compatibility with the potential cast around them** — who do they work well with, who do they have chemistry with, how do they relate to others, where do they best fit the ensemble?
* **Behavior and attitude in auditions and callbacks**. Were they talking when someone else was performing? Did they listen and respond to other actors and to staff? Did they demonstrate curiosity and a willingness to work?
* **Calendar conflicts** — how many rehearsals the actor can commit to. Our productions and their schedules are challenging, and people who miss rehearsals will not keep up.
* **Requested carpool arrangements.**

Ensemble roles can get a bad rap elsewhere, but this is not our position at PYT! We know they are critical to every performance, which is why we celebrate an ensemble member with our biggest award, every show.

Above all, it's crucial for any actor and any parent preparing to register or audition for a PYT show, to discuss and understand that the decision is:"will it be fun to be a part of this show, no matter the role?"
closing_quote: Each show, we celebrate our performers with the Judy Robe & Spirits Awards.
